channels.label: Mukhyamantri Seva Sankalp Helpline (CM Helpline)
channels.source_name: Department of Digital Technologies and Governance, Government of Himachal Pradesh
channels.note: Himachal Pradesh's own CM helpline - toll-free, one-stop line for public grievances, demands and suggestions. Call centre at Shimla, operational all days 7:00 am to 10:00 pm. Also linked as 'Mukhyamantri Seva Sankalp Helpline 1100' from the state portal homepage.

channels.label: Mukhyamantri Seva Sankalp - CM grievance portal
channels.source_name: Mukhyamantri Seva Sankalp, Government of Himachal Pradesh
channels.note: Official HP grievance portal tied to helpline 1100. Register grievances, track status, receive SMS/email updates and give feedback on resolution.

channels.label: eSamadhan - Online Public Grievance Monitoring System
channels.source_name: Department of Redressal of Public Grievances, HP Secretariat
channels.note: HP's grievance monitoring system, run by the Department of Redressal of Public Grievances, HP Secretariat, Shimla. Covers grievances against HP government organisations; directs central/other-state matters to CPGRAMS. Linked from the state portal homepage as 'eSamadhan - Grievance Redressal'.

channels.label: HP Police Citizen Portal
channels.source_name: Himachal Pradesh Police (HP CCTNS)
channels.note: Official HP Police citizen portal (Project HP CCTNS). Online complaint, citizen tip, view FIR, FIR/complaint status search, tenant/employee/domestic-help verification, police clearance and character certificates, traffic challan. Note: the domain root (citizenportal.hppolice.gov.in/) returns 404 - this /citizen/login.htm path is the working landing page. The site's TLS chain omits an intermediate certificate, so some clients warn; the certificate itself is valid and issued to 'Himachal Pradesh Police'.

channels.label: Police (To Report a Crime - Control Room)
channels.source_name: Government of Himachal Pradesh - official state portal (Helpline page)
channels.note: Published as 'Police' on the state portal helpline page and as 'To Report a Crime - Control Room' in the HP Police Citizen Portal telephone directory.

channels.label: State Police Control Room, Shimla
channels.source_name: HP Police Citizen Portal - Telephone Directory
channels.note: Published as 'State Police Control Room Shimla' with lines (0177) 2621711, 2621618, 2621723, 2621736, 2621714, 2621614, 2621746, 2621796. The PHQ Control Room at Nigam Vihar, Shimla is separately listed under I/C State Control Room as 0177-2621614.

channels.label: Gudiya Helpline (women)
channels.source_name: Government of Himachal Pradesh - official state portal (Helpline page)
channels.note: Himachal-specific helpline published by the state government. Listed on the state portal's helpline page alongside Women Helpline 1091 and Child Helpline 1098, and in the HP Police Citizen Portal telephone directory. Also listed on district sites (e.g. Shimla, Una, Chamba). Official pages list the number without an explicit stated scope; it was launched as a women's safety helpline alongside the Shakti women-safety app.

channels.label: Hoshiyar Singh Helpline / Crime Stopper
channels.source_name: Government of Himachal Pradesh - official state portal (Helpline page)
channels.note: Himachal-specific. The state portal helpline page lists 1090 twice - as 'Crime Stopper' and as 'Hoshiyar Singh Helpline' - indicating it is a crime-information reporting line. Also listed in the HP Police Citizen Portal telephone directory and on district sites (Shimla, Una, Chamba).

channels.label: Cyber Crime Cell (Himachal Pradesh)
channels.source_name: Government of Himachal Pradesh - official state portal (Helpline page)
channels.note: State cyber crime cell number published on the state portal helpline page as 'Cyber Crime Cell: 1930, 0177-2620331'. The HP Police telephone directory lists the same number under 'Report Cyber Crime' and for Dy.SP Cyber Crime PS SR Shimla. (1930 is the national NCRP line.)

channels.label: Report Against Corruption - Vigilance & Anti-Corruption Bureau
channels.source_name: Government of Himachal Pradesh - official state portal (Helpline page)
channels.note: Published as 'Report Against Corruption' on the state portal helpline page and as 'Vigilance & Anti-Corruption Bureau' in the HP Police Citizen Portal telephone directory. The police directory additionally lists 'Report Corruption through WhatsApp: 8988700100'.

channels.label: Disaster Management Control Room (Himachal Pradesh)
channels.source_name: Government of Himachal Pradesh - official state portal (Helpline page)
channels.note: Published on the state portal helpline page as 'Disaster Management Control Room: 0177-2929688, 2629439, 2629939, 2628940'.

channels.label: HPSEBL Electricity Complaint Helpline
channels.source_name: Himachal Pradesh State Electricity Board Limited (HPSEBL)
channels.note: Published on the HPSEBL homepage as 'Helpline: 1800-180-8060 / 1912'. HPSEBL is Himachal's state electricity distribution utility; its Consumer Call Centre operates 24x7. General email: info@hpseb.in.

channels.label: HPSEBL Toll-Free Consumer Helpline
channels.source_name: Himachal Pradesh State Electricity Board Limited (HPSEBL)
channels.note: Published on the HPSEBL homepage as 'Helpline: 1800-180-8060 / 1912'. A separate pension-only line, 0177-2658908 (working days 10 am to 5 pm), is listed for pension queries.

channels.label: HPSEBL Consumer Call Centre
channels.source_name: Himachal Pradesh State Electricity Board Limited (HPSEBL)
channels.note: HPSEBL's Consumer Call Centre page describing its 24x7 complaint handling. The page itself describes the service but does not print the numbers - those are published on the HPSEBL homepage (1800-180-8060 / 1912).

channels.label: Government of Himachal Pradesh - official state portal
channels.source_name: Government of Himachal Pradesh - official state portal
channels.note: The official state web portal ('This is the official web portal of the Himachal Pradesh Government, India'), developed and hosted by NIC Himachal Pradesh State Centre. HP Secretariat, Shimla: +91-177-2622204 / +91-177-2621154. Note: the widely-indexed helpline URL himachal.nic.in/en-IN/helpline.html is dead (redirects to an error page); the live helpline listing is at himachal.nic.in/Content/HtmlPage?qs=LdEF%2BAuYkB7rMRolWuVbNg%3D%3D.

channels.label: District Portal of Himachal Pradesh - district directory
channels.source_name: Government of Himachal Pradesh - District Portal (S3WaaS/NIC)
channels.note: Official 'District Portal of Himachal Pradesh' listing and linking all 12 district websites (Bilaspur, Chamba, Hamirpur, Kangra, Kinnaur, Kullu, Lahaul and Spiti, Mandi, Shimla, Sirmaur, Solan, Una), each with English and Hindi options. District sites follow the hp<district>.nic.in pattern (e.g. hpshimla.nic.in, hpuna.nic.in, hpchamba.nic.in), and each publishes its own /helpline/ page.

channels.label: National Emergency Number (ERSS) - Police, Fire & Rescue, Health
channels.source_name: Ministry of Home Affairs, Government of India
channels.note: MHA's single pan-India emergency number: 'ONE INDIA ONE EMERGENCY NUMBER 112', a nationwide unified emergency response system. Works in every State/UT. Corroborated by india.gov.in helpline directory, which lists 112 as the Integrated Helpline (ERSS) covering Police, Fire & Rescue and Health services.

channels.label: Fire Helpline
channels.source_name: National Portal of India (india.gov.in), NIC / MeitY
channels.note: Listed under 'Emergency Numbers' on the National Portal of India helpline directory (page last reviewed/updated 14 July 2026).

channels.label: National Ambulance Service (NAS)
channels.source_name: National Portal of India (india.gov.in), NIC / MeitY
channels.note: Listed as 'National Ambulance Service (NAS)' under Emergency Numbers on the National Portal of India helpline directory (page last reviewed/updated 14 July 2026).

channels.label: Women Helpline (national, toll-free)
channels.source_name: Ministry of Women and Child Development, Government of India
channels.note: Published as 'Women Helpline Toll free No. - 181' directly by the owning ministry (WCD) as a tel: link on its homepage. Also listed on india.gov.in as both 'Women Helpline Number' and 'Domestic Violence Helpline'.

channels.label: National Commission for Women (NCW) 24x7 Helpline
channels.source_name: National Commission for Women (NCW)
channels.note: NCW's own site states: 'Call the 24x7 NCW helpline at 14490 for immediate assistance and support', and lists 'NCW Women Helpline (14490)' under its Complaint Redressal cells. IMPORTANT: the india.gov.in helpline directory still lists 7827170170 as the 'National Commission for Women Helpline' - that number appears nowhere on ncw.gov.in (not even in raw HTML), so it appears stale and was deliberately omitted in favour of the department's own current number.

channels.label: Child Helpline (national, toll-free)
channels.source_name: Ministry of Women and Child Development, Government of India
channels.note: Published as 'Child Helpline Toll free No. - 1098' directly by the owning ministry (WCD) as a tel: link on its homepage. Also listed as 'Child Help Line' on india.gov.in.

channels.label: Elderline - National Helpline for Senior Citizens (NHSC)
channels.source_name: Department of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of India
channels.note: Published as 'Elder Helpline: 14567' on the Department of Social Justice & Empowerment's own Elderline site, which the ministry homepage links to as the 'National Helpline for Senior Citizens (NHSC)'. india.gov.in additionally lists a landline 011-20893999 alongside 14567 for the Senior Citizens Helpline.

channels.label: National Consumer Helpline (NCH)
channels.source_name: National Portal of India (india.gov.in), NIC / MeitY
channels.note: Listed as 'National Consumer Helpline (NCH)' on the National Portal of India helpline directory (page last reviewed/updated 14 July 2026). Note: the NCH portal consumerhelpline.gov.in could not be reached at time of research (DNS resolves to an NIC address but the host refused connections / returned 504), so only the number is cited here - not the NCH portal URL.

channels.label: Cyber Crime Helpline (online financial fraud reporting)
channels.source_name: National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal, MHA / I4C
channels.note: Confirmed on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal homepage banner (MHA / Indian Cyber Crime Coordination Centre - I4C), which reads: 'To report online financial fraud, call 1930' and 'register your complaint at cybercrime.gov.in'. Independently corroborated by the india.gov.in helpline directory, which lists 1930 as the Cyber Crime Helpline.

channels.label: National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal (file a cybercrime complaint)
channels.source_name: National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal, MHA / I4C
channels.note: Verified live. Ministry of Home Affairs portal for reporting cybercrime; supports anonymous reporting, women/children-related crime, financial fraud and other cybercrime, plus complaint tracking. Nationwide.

channels.label: CPGRAMS - Centralised Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System
channels.source_name: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances & Pensions, Government of India
channels.note: Verified live; this is the citizen-facing portal. The site states CPGRAMS is 'an online platform available to the citizens 24x7 to lodge their grievances to the public authorities on any subject related to service delivery... a single portal connected to all the Ministries/Departments of Government of India and States.' Supports tracking by registration ID and an appeal facility. Note: it does not cover RTI matters, subjudice/court matters, or religious matters.

channels.label: Central Vigilance Commission - lodge a corruption complaint
channels.source_name: Central Vigilance Commission (CVC), Government of India
channels.note: Verified live. CVC's Complaint Management System with a 'Citizen Login', complaint status tracking and a published complaint handling policy. Linked from cvc.gov.in as 'Lodge a complaint'. Covers corruption complaints against central government organisations.

channels.label: Relief Commissioner for Natural Calamities
channels.source_name: National Portal of India (india.gov.in), NIC / MeitY
channels.note: Listed on the National Portal of India helpline directory (page last reviewed/updated 14 July 2026).

channels.label: Road Accident Helpline
channels.source_name: National Portal of India (india.gov.in), NIC / MeitY
channels.note: Listed on the National Portal of India helpline directory (page last reviewed/updated 14 July 2026).
